# We Don't Cut Corners, We Pour Them

## We Don’t Just Pour Concrete. We Custom Tailor to Your Metal Building.

A strong building is only as good as its solid foundation. Here at WolfSteel, we consistently work with local engineers throughout Texas to build foundations that will stand the test of time.

Texas has a BIG problem with  unstable clay soils, creating difficulties for foundation engineers.  We tailor our foundations based on your specific needs, considering factors like rebar types, concrete strength, [slab](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-cl0ucTKBjE&t=11s) thickness, and footing design. Different buildings, like Tubular and Red Iron, require different foundation types.

Concrete services offered in select areas and only for our metal building with concrete projects.

[Learn More](https://wolfsteelbuildings.com/why-wolfsteel/)

## Quality is in the Details

What makes WolfSteel concrete unique is our personalized approach to customizing concrete details for your building needs. The #1 problem in the industry is that concrete isn’t poured to the specifications of the metal building which can lead to frustrating leaks under panels, garage doors, and lean-tos. For instance, a door notch detail for a tubular building is not the same for a red iron building. Unfortunately, little to no communication happens between concrete and building contractors.

As a turnkey company, we focus on designing foundations that fit your specific metal building, making our concrete foundation notch detailing effective.

Click [here](https://wolfsteelbuildings.com/warranty#tab2) to see our concrete warranty coverage.

![Concrete for Texas Metal Buildings](<Base64-Image-Removed>)Concrete for Texas Metal Buildings

## More than just Foundations

## Don't Skip the Prep

When it comes to your Texas metal building  do not skip your site preparation.

### What is Site Preparation for Metal Buildings?

Site preparation is exactly what it seems… Preparing a site for the construction of a building through various steps. Site Prep is essential in having a lasting foundation and can save you from any headaches down the line.

The specific steps involved in the preparation can vary depending on the metal building size, type, and the local regulations.

Site preparation costs can only be determined after assessing your site.

![Site Preparation](<Base64-Image-Removed>)Site Preparation for Metal Buildings

### Why Site Preparation Matters?

Site Prep makes sure that the ground beneath your foundation and building is stable and secure. Without proper site prep, even the strongest building can face significant problems that lead to costly repairs.

Key Steps to Effective Site Preparation

- Site assessment
- Site clearing and excavation
- Grading and drainage
- Soil compaction

## Additional Services Available

### Demolition

### Engineered Foundations

### Driveways, sidewalks, and approaches

### pump rentals for driveway protection

### drain systems and stem walls

## Frequently Asked Questions

What is included in your concrete work?

Our concrete work includes the following:

- The concrete foundation _(min. 3500 PSI)_
- Perimeter and internal footings
- Rebar for a more reinforced slab
- Vapor barrier _(commonly not used)_
- Notch edge detail on the perimeter and on doors prevent water exposure concerns.
- Control Joints _(unless removed by you)_
- Broom or Trowel finishing
- Weld Plates and Squares for Red Iron Metal Buildings.
- Concrete Squares _(Added for red iron buildings and for slabs with lift)_
- Pier _(for ground conditions with extremely soft soil or very unstable ground)_

What is included in site prep?

Site preparation includes but isn’t limited to:

- Demolition
- Excavating
- Clearing vegetation
- Building pads _(select fill or road base)_
- Foundation Layout _(Marking perimeter with string lines, stakes, and leveling tools for accurate dimensions)_
- Framework installation _(Setting wood frames to create the mold)_
- Grading and Drainage
- Culverts

How much would site preparation cost me?

The cost may vary depending on your location and the condition of your land. A site visit will be required to fully calculate dirt work, extent of clearing/level site, and the cost.

Learn more about WolfSteel site prep cost and why we are not cutting corners [here.](https://wolfsteelbuildings.com/site-prep-cost-explained-why-wolfsteel-isnt-cutting-corners/)

I’ll do my own concrete and site preparation. Can you still install my metal building?

Yes, but waivers will need to be signed.

- A concrete waiver to acknowledge that WolfSteel Buildings will not be liable for any damages to the structure due to potential foundation issues.
- A site prep waiver to acknowledge that WolfSteel Buildings will not be liable for any deficiencies in grading, compaction, soil, etc.
